name: Leadership in the Business World

cost.notes: Need-based scholarships available; limited full scholarships

prior_knowledge: Open to all backgrounds; interest in business and leadership

essays.prompt: Describe a time when you demonstrated leadership and what you learned from the experience.
tips: Wharton looks for authentic leadership experiences — don't conflate leadership with authority
Show what you learned, not just what you accomplished

outcomes.college_impact: Positive signal at Wharton and other top business schools (Kellogg, Sloan, CBS). LBW alumni are well-prepared for Wharton-style case discussions.
outcomes.typical_results: Most LBW alumni attend Wharton or other top business programs; strong representation at Penn overall.

what_they_look_for: Demonstrated leadership in school or community
Intellectual curiosity about business and economics

blurbs.short: A 3-week intensive summer business program at Wharton where rising juniors and seniors develop leadership and entrepreneurial skills through case studies and projects.
blurbs.long: LBW (Leadership in the Business World) is Wharton's official summer program for high school students. The program exposes students to Wharton's case-based teaching method — the same approach used in Wharton's MBA program — where students analyze real business situations in small groups. The curriculum covers entrepreneurship, finance, marketing, management, and leadership. Beyond the classroom, students participate in site visits to Philadelphia-area companies, network with Wharton alumni, and develop a final business plan. LBW alumni who apply to Penn have historically had a meaningful admissions advantage — Penn admissions officers know the program. However, LBW is valuable independent of college admissions: it genuinely gives students a window into what a top business education feels like.

curriculum: Wharton case method, lectures from Wharton faculty, team projects, business plan development, company site visits, networking events.
